item.title: F-013: Render, Print, and Share
item.intent: Share markdown with the world — as a .md file via share sheet, as a beautiful print, or as a rendered PDF. Markdown is the primary format; rendering is for handoff to non-markdown users.
item.description: Share as markdown: iOS share sheet for .md file (AirDrop, email, Messages, any app). Save to new location: copy .md to different directory/cloud/external storage. Print: rendered rich text matching in-editor appearance. Rendered PDF: polished PDF for sharing with non-markdown users. No Word, Pages, RTF, or proprietary format export (D-NO-12).
acceptanceCriteria: User exports a document with headings, lists, code blocks, and images to PDF — result looks like a polished document
PDF export includes images from relative paths
User can save/copy the .md file to a different directory or cloud drive
User shares .md file via AirDrop to a colleague
User prints document and output matches in-editor rich text appearance
Rendered PDF includes images and code blocks with syntax highlighting
No proprietary format options appear anywhere in the export flow (no Word, Pages, RTF, HTML)
